name: Cranberry Sauce (Smooth)
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Artificial sweetener (e.g., aspartame or liquid sweetener)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, heat the cranberries and water until they reach a simmer.
2. Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the berries have burst and the liquid has a syrup-like consistency.
3. Strain the mixture through a sieve, discarding the berry skins.
4. To the hot liquid, add the gelatin, sweetener, and blue food coloring (if using), stirring until dissolved.
5. Pour the mixture into a 3-cup gelatin mold and chill overnight.
6. This recipe makes 12 servings of 1/4 cup each, with approximately 11 calories per serving.
NOTE: The reason for adding blue food coloring is that without it, this cranberry sauce appears very red. To achieve a purplish hue similar to canned cranberry sauce, add a drop or two of blue food coloring.
